---
title: "Estimating Biomoss Loss from Pagami Creek Fire"
author: "Alexander Graeff"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

<style>
pre {
  overflow-x: auto;
}
pre code {
  white-space: pre; /* Prevents wrapping, forces horizontal scroll */
}
</style>

```{r Setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  fig.width = 10,        # Default plotting width (inches)
  fig.height = 6,        # Default plotting height
  out.width = "100%",    # Fit figure to page width in output
  fig.align = "center"   # Center figuresecho = TRUE)
)

Sys.setlocale("LC_CTYPE", "English_United States.utf8")

```

```{r, echo = FALSE, results="hide", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, out.extra='style="margin-bottom:-20px;"'}
```

```{r Clean Slate, include=FALSE}

  graphics.off()
  rm(list=ls())
  gc()

```

```{r Load Packages, include=FALSE}

  library(tidyverse)
  library(readxl)
  library(here)
  library(rlang)

```

# Goal

Summarize biomass data per forest type for reference and burn plots: calculate 
an average biomass per forest type and then take the difference between 
reference plots and burns plots within a forest type to estimate the amount of 
biomass lost from the burn. 

# Create Formulas for Repeated Code

```{r Formulas}

  group_count <- function(data, ...) {
    data %>% 
      group_by(...) %>% 
        summarize(n = n(), .groups = "keep") %>% 
        ungroup() %>% 
      pivot_wider(names_from = ref_burn, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)
  }

```

# Import Data

Biomass calculations in the raw biomass data were made by Sue Lietz. Metadata is 
included in the file, but not all supporting data for biomass calculations are 
supplied in this directory. For more information about biomass calculations, 
contact the author.

forestTypes were generated with scripts/forestTypes.R

severity was generated in a different repository, but is essentially a 
compilation of plot names, crown burn severity, locations, and forest type 
classifications (different than the forestTypes imported separately).

```{r Import Data}

# biomass data ----

  biomass.raw <- read_xls(path = here("data/raw/pagami_DOMspecies_bio20131210.xls"), 
                      sheet = "DOM_spp")

  head(biomass.raw)
  
# forest types ----

  forestTypes <- read_csv(file = here("data/reference/forestTypes.csv"), 
                          show_col_types = FALSE,
                          locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8")) %>% 
    rename(forestType_code = code,  # rename some things so they are more understandable below
           forestType = name, 
           forestType_group = group)
  
  head(forestTypes)

# severity ----

  severity <- read_csv(file = here("data/reference/severityCrn_forestType_plot.csv"), 
                       show_col_types = FALSE) 
  
  head(severity)

```

# Format Data

Here we pare down the biomass table and join the reference tables, then select 
columns relevant to the analyses. This is saved in data/processed as it is the 
data frame used to perform downstream analyses.

```{r Format Data}

  data <- biomass.raw %>% 
    select(1, 4) %>%  # just take the total biomass and plot ID columns
    rename(tpIdent = 1, biomassTotal = 2) %>%  # rename them
    left_join(forestTypes, join_by(tpIdent)) %>%  # join the forest types data
    left_join(severity %>%  # join the severity data
                select(TP_IDENT, ref_burn, SeverityIndex, contains("crn")),  # (just a subset of it)
              join_by(tpIdent == TP_IDENT)) %>% 
    select(tpIdent:ref_burn, crnSev)

  head(data)

```

```{r Write Processed Data Table, include=FALSE}

  data %>% 
    rename(crownBurnSeverity = crnSev,
           "biomassTotal (mg/ha)" = biomassTotal) %>% 
    write_csv(file = here("data/processed/biomassData_processed.csv"))

```

# Check Group Sizes

At a minimum, for biomass comparisons, we need one reference plot in each forest 
type for which there are burn plots.

Indeed we do have at least one reference plot for each burn plot forest tyoe, 
however the sample sizes are pretty small where many have just 1.

```{r Check Group Sizes - Forest Type, rows.print=13}

(d <- data %>% 
    group_count(ref_burn, forestType) %>% 
    rename(burnPlots = burn, referencePlots = reference))
  
```

```{r Check Group Sizes - Forest Type - Write, include=FALSE}

  write_csv(x = d, file = here("output/tables/sampleSizes_forestType.csv"))

  rm(d)

```

Let's look at the forest type groups as well.

There are 3 groups with pretty good sample sizes, and a couple with still-low 
sample sizes. Of course, with this, we lose a lot of forest type detail.

```{r Check Group Sizes - Forest Type Groups}

  (d <- data %>% 
    group_count(ref_burn, forestType_group) %>% 
    rename(burnPlots = burn, referencePlots = reference))

```

```{r Check Group Sizes - Forest Type Groups - Write, include=FALSE}

  write_csv(x = d, file = here("output/tables/sampleSizes_forestTypeGroups.csv"))

  rm(d)

```

For reference, we'll generate tables with crown burn severity as well, since 
these plot counts are difficult to display on the charts which include crown 
burn severity.

For forest type...

```{r Check Group Sizes - Forest Type and Crown Burn Severity}

  (d <- data %>% 
    group_count(ref_burn, forestType, crnSev) %>% 
    rename(burnPlots = burn, referencePlots = reference,
           crownBurnSeverity = crnSev))

```

```{r Check Group Sizes - Forest Type and Crown Burn Severity - Write, include=FALSE}

  write_csv(x = d, file = here("output/tables/sampleSizes_forestTypeBurnSeverity.csv"))

  rm(d)

```

and forest type groups.

```{r Check Group Sizes - Forest Type Groups and Crown Burn Severity}

  (d <- data %>% 
    group_count(ref_burn, forestType_group, crnSev) %>% 
    rename(burnPlots = burn, referencePlots = reference,
           crownBurnSeverity = crnSev))

```

```{r Check Group Sizes - Forest Type Groups and Crown Burn Severity - Write, include=FALSE}

  write_csv(x = d, file = here("output/tables/sampleSizes_forestTypeGroupsBurnSeverity.csv"))

  rm(d)

```

# Calulate Biomass Differences in Groups

First, we will calculate by subtracting the average reference plot biomass 
within each forest type from each individual burn plot in that forest type. Then 
we can average the burn plots within each forest type.

We can calculate this a couple of different ways, but we should end up with 
roughly the same answer.

An intermediate step in this code allows us to see the estimated biomass lost 
individually for each plot. Otherwise, the calculations are probably more 
convoluted than the next step (Calculation B). But, it is more flexible as 
we will want to summarize by different groupings and may want to 
calculate averages directly, or leave plots with individual values (for use in 
boxplots).


```{r Biomass Differences - Calculation A - Formula}

  biomass_diff_a <- function(data, x, y = NULL, sev_col = NULL, calculation) {  # define function with required x, optional y and sev_col
    sev_quo <- enquo(sev_col)  # capture sev_col as quosure (tidy-eval)
    has_sev <- !quo_is_null(sev_quo)  # flag: TRUE if sev_col was supplied
  
    a <- data %>%
      select(tpIdent, biomassTotal, forestType,  # keep needed columns
             forestType_group, ref_burn, crnSev) %>%
      group_by({{x}}) %>%  # group by primary variable x
        mutate(biomassMean_ref = mean(biomassTotal[ref_burn == "reference"])) %>%  # calculate mean biomass of reference plots within x
        ungroup() %>%  # remove grouping
      group_by({{x}}, {{y}}) %>%  # group by primary variable x and secondary variable y (if given)
        mutate(biomassDiff = if_else(condition = ref_burn == "burn",  # for burn rows, calculate biomassDiff...
                                     true = biomassMean_ref - biomassTotal,  # as ref mean biomass minus individual plot biomass
                                     false = NA)) %>%
        ungroup() # remove grouping
  
        if (calculation == "average") { a %>%  # if we want to calculate the calculation means across groups then
              filter(ref_burn == "burn") %>%  # keep only burn rows
              { 
                if (has_sev) filter(., !is.na(!!sev_quo)) else .  # and if we're including crown severity then remove plots which don't have a crown severity
                } %>%  # optionally require non-missing sev_col
              group_by({{x}}, {{y}}) %>%  # group by x and y
                summarize(meanDiff = mean(biomassDiff), .groups = "keep") %>%  # compute mean difference per group (consider na.rm = TRUE)
                ungroup() %>%  # remove grouping
              arrange({{x}}, {{y}})
          }  # order by x then y
        else if (calculation == "unaveraged") { a %>%  # otherwise, we won't perform any calculations...
          filter(ref_burn != "reference") %>%  #... but we will remove all reference plots and...
          {
          if (has_sev) filter(., #... if we are summarizing by crown burn severity... 
                              !is.na(!!sev_quo))  #... then we will remove any plots which don't have a severity value
          else .
          }
        }
        else if (calculation == "intermediate") {
          a
        }
      } # end function

```

Example of Calculation A output.

```{r Calculation A - Example}

  data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType, 
                   y = NULL,
                   sev_col = NULL,
                   calculation  = "average")

```

If we average biomass for burn and reference plots, then subtract, it should be 
just about the same as the calculation above.

This code is probably more intuitive, (and is much simpler) but doesn't include 
the separation of reference calculation so we can't easily add crown severity as 
a grouping variable.

Indeed we get the same values, which is a good check, but we want the more 
flexible calculation going forward - so we'll just use Calculation A.

```{r Biomass Differences - Calculation B - Formula}
  
  biomass_diff_b <- function(data, ...) {
    data %>% 
      group_by(...) %>% 
        summarize(biomassMean = mean(biomassTotal),
                  .groups = "keep") %>% 
        ungroup() %>% 
      pivot_wider(names_from = ref_burn, 
                  names_glue = "{ref_burn}_biomass",
                  values_from = biomassMean, 
                  values_fill = 0) %>% 
      mutate(meanBiomassDiff = reference_biomass - burn_biomass)
  }

```

Example Calculation B output.

```{r Calculation B - Example}

  data %>% 
    biomass_diff_b(ref_burn, forestType)

```

# Save Data Tables

We will hard save the summarized data tables as companions to the visualized 
data below.

```{r Save Data Tables}

# biomass loss for each plot ----

  data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType, 
                   y = NULL,
                   sev_col = NULL,
                   calculation  = "intermediate") %>% 
    rename("biomassLoss (mg/ha)" = biomassDiff,
           crownBurnSeverity = crnSev,
           "biomassMean_ref (mg/ha)" = biomassMean_ref) %>% 
  write_csv(file = here("output/tables/biomassLoss_plot.csv"))

# average biomass differences from reference plots in forest types ----

  data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType, 
                   y = NULL,
                   sev_col = NULL,
                   calculation  = "average") %>% 
    rename("mean_biomassLoss (mg/ha)" = meanDiff) %>% 
  write_csv(file = here("output/tables/biomassLoss_forestType.csv"))

# average biomass differences from reference plots in forest type groups ----

  data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType_group, 
                   y = NULL,
                   sev_col = NULL,
                   calculation  = "average") %>% 
    rename("mean_biomassLoss (mg/ha)" = meanDiff) %>% 
    write_csv(file = here("output/tables/biomassLoss_forestTypeGroup.csv"))
  

# average biomass differences from reference plots in forest type by crown burn severity ----

  data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType, 
                   y = crnSev,
                   sev_col = crnSev,
                   calculation  = "average") %>% 
    rename("mean_biomassLoss (mg/ha)" = meanDiff,
           crownBurnSeverity = crnSev) %>% 
    write_csv(file = here("output/tables/biomassLoss_forestTypeGroupBurnSeverity.csv"))


# average biomass differences from reference plots in forest type groups by crown burn severity ----

  data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType_group, 
                   y = crnSev,
                   sev_col = crnSev,
                   calculation  = "average") %>% 
    rename("mean_biomassLoss (mg/ha)" = meanDiff,
           crownBurnSeverity = crnSev) %>% 
    write_csv(file = here("output/tables/biomassLoss_forestTypeGroupBurnSeverity.csv"))


```


# Visualize

For visualization, we summarize the data for plotting using the biomass_diff_a 
function.

```{r Plotting Functions, include=FALSE}

  n_labs <- function(data, ...) {
    data %>% 
      group_by(...) %>% 
        summarize(numPlots = n(), .groups = "keep") %>% 
       ungroup() 
    }

  n_labs_plotter <- function(x, sourceY, yCorr, size) {
    yMin <- pData %>% 
      summarize(minVal = min({{sourceY}}))
    
      geom_text(data = nLabs,
                aes(x = {{x}}, 
                    y = yMin$minVal - yCorr, 
                    label = paste0("n=", numPlots)),
                    inherit.aes = FALSE,
                size = size)
  }

```

```{r Plotting Preferences, include=FALSE}
  
  severity.colors <- setNames(
    c("#00A65A", "#FFD300", "#FF7F11", "#D7263D"),
    c(1:4))

```

## By Forest Type

### Column Chart

```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type - Calculation A}

# generate a table to label number of plots ----

  nLabs <- data %>% 
    n_labs(forestType, ref_burn) %>% 
    filter(ref_burn == "burn")

# perform calculations ----

  pData <- data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType, 
                   y = NULL,
                   sev_col = NULL,
                   calculation  = "average")
  
  pData
  
# plot ----
  
  pData %>% 
    ggplot(mapping = aes(x = forestType, y = meanDiff, fill = forestType)) +
      geom_col() +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
            axis.line.y = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
      labs(y = "Average Biomass Loss (mg/ha)",
           fill = "Forest Type") +
      n_labs_plotter(x = forestType,
                     sourceY = meanDiff,
                     yCorr = 4,
                     size = 3)
  
```
Figure 1. Average biomass loss across plots in each of several forest types, 
calculated as the average biomass in burn plots in each forest type subtracted 
from the average biomass in reference plots in each forest type.  Forest types 
were calculated following Forest Inventory and Analysis guidelines, but using 
biomass as a proxy. Labels below each bar indicate the number of plots in each 
group. 

```{r Remove Plotting Dfs 1, include=FALSE}

  rm(nLabs, pData)

```

```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type - Write, include=FALSE}

  ggsave(filename = here("output/figures/biomassLoss_forestType_col.jpg"),
         width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 600)

```

### Box Plot

For boxplots, we skip the averaging step in the function.

```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type - Calculation A - Boxplot}

# generate a table to label number of plots ----

  nLabs <- data %>% 
    n_labs(forestType, ref_burn) %>% 
    filter(ref_burn == "burn")

# perform calculations ----

  pData <- data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType, 
                   y = NULL,
                   sev_col = NULL,
                   calculation  = "unaveraged") 
  
  pData
  
# plot ----
  
  pData %>%  
    ggplot(mapping = aes(x = forestType, y = biomassDiff, fill = forestType)) +
      geom_boxplot() +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.line.y = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
            axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, 
                                       vjust = 1, 
                                       hjust = 1, 
                                       size = rel(.9)),
            legend.position = "none") +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
      labs(y = "Biomass Loss (mg/ha)",
           x = "Forest Type") +
      n_labs_plotter(x = forestType,
                     sourceY = biomassDiff,
                     yCorr = 15,
                     size = 3)

```
Figure 2. Biomass loss across plots in each of several forest types, calculated 
as the biomass in each burn plot in each forest type subtracted from the average 
biomass in reference plots in each forest type.  Forest types were calculated 
following Forest Inventory and Analysis guidelines, but using biomass as a 
proxy. Labels below each bar indicate the number of plots in each group. 

```{r Remove Plotting Dfs 2, include=FALSE}

  rm(nLabs, pData)

```

```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type - Boxplot - Write, include=FALSE}

  ggsave(filename = here("output/figures/biomassLoss_forestType_box.jpg"),
         width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 600)

```

## By Forest Type Groups

We'll do the same thing as above but with the forest type groups.

### Column Chart

```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type Groups - Calculation A}

# generate a table to label number of plots ----

  nLabs <- data %>% 
    n_labs(forestType_group, ref_burn) %>% 
    filter(ref_burn == "burn")

# perform calculations ----

  pData <- data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType_group, 
                   y = NULL,
                   sev_col = NULL,
                   calculation  = "average") 
  
  pData
  
# plot ----

  pData %>% 
    ggplot(mapping = aes(x = forestType_group, 
                         y = meanDiff, 
                         fill = forestType_group)) +
      geom_col() +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
            axis.line.y = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
      labs(y = "Average Biomass Loss (mg/ha)",
           fill = "Forest Type Group") + 
      n_labs_plotter(x = forestType_group,
                     sourceY = meanDiff,
                     yCorr = 4,
                     size = 3)
  
```
Figure 3. Average biomass loss across plots in forest types groups calculated as 
the average biomass in burn plots in each forest type group subtracted from the 
average biomass in reference plots in each forest type group.  Forest type groups
were calculated following Forest Inventory and Analysis guidelines, but using 
biomass as a proxy. Labels below each bar indicate the number of plots in each 
group. 

```{r Remove Plotting Dfs 3, include=FALSE}

  rm(nLabs, pData)

```

```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type Groups - Write, include=FALSE}

  ggsave(filename = here("output/figures/biomassLoss_forestTypeGroups_col.jpg"),
         width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 600)

```

### Box Plot

```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type Groups - Calculation A - Boxplot}

# generate a table to label number of plots ----

  nLabs <- data %>% 
    n_labs(forestType_group, ref_burn) %>% 
    filter(ref_burn == "burn")

# perform calculations ----

  pData <- data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType_group, 
                   y = NULL,
                   sev_col = NULL,
                   calculation  = "unaveraged")

  pData
  
# plot ----

  pData %>% 
    ggplot(mapping = aes(x = forestType_group, 
                         y = biomassDiff, 
                         fill = forestType_group)) +
      geom_boxplot() +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.line.y = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
            axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, 
                                       vjust = 1, 
                                       hjust = 1,
                                       size = rel(.9)),
            legend.position = "none") +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
      labs(y = "Average Biomass Loss (mg/ha)",
           x = "Forest Type Group")  +
        n_labs_plotter(x = forestType_group,
                       sourceY = biomassDiff,
                       yCorr = 15,
                       size = 3)
  
```
Figure 4. Biomass loss across plots in forest type groups, calculated as the 
biomass in each burn plot in each forest type group subtracted from the
average biomass in reference plots in each forest type. Forest type groups were 
calculated following Forest Inventory and Analysis guidelines, but using biomass 
as a proxy. Labels below each bar indicate the number of plots in each group. 

```{r Remove Plotting Dfs 4, include=FALSE}

  rm(nLabs, pData)

```

```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type Groups - Boxplot - Write, include=FALSE}

  ggsave(filename = here("output/figures/biomassLoss_forestTypeGroups_box.jpg"),
         width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 600)

```

## By Forest Type and Crown Burn Severity

```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type and Crown Burn Severity - Calculation A}

# perform calculations ----

  pData <- data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType,
                   y = crnSev,
                   sev_col = crnSev,
                   calculation  = "average")

  pData
  
# plot ----

  pData %>% 
    ggplot(mapping = aes(x = forestType, 
                         y = meanDiff, 
                         fill = as.character(crnSev))) +
      geom_col(position = position_dodge(preserve = "single")) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = severity.colors) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, 
                                       vjust = 1, 
                                       hjust = 1,
                                       size = rel(.9))) +
    labs(x = "Forest Type",
         y = "Average Biomass Loss (mg/ha)",
         fill = "Crown Burn Severity")
  
```
Figure 5. Average biomass loss across plots in each of several forest types and 
by plot crown burn severity, calculated as the biomass of each group subtracted 
from the average biomass in reference plots for each group. Forest types were 
calculated following Forest Inventory and Analysis guidelines, but using biomass 
as a proxy.

```{r Remove Plotting Dfs 5, include=FALSE}

 rm(pData)

```


```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type and Crown Burn Severity - Write, include=FALSE}

  ggsave(filename = here("output/figures/biomassLoss_forestTypeBurnSeverity_col.jpg"),
         width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 600)

```

## By Forest Type and Crown Burn Severity

```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type Groups and Crown Burn Severity - Calculation A}

# perform calculations ----

  pData <- data %>% 
    biomass_diff_a(x = forestType_group,
                   y = crnSev,
                   sev_col = crnSev,
                   calculation  = "average")

  pData
  
# plot ----

  pData %>% 
    ggplot(mapping = aes(x = forestType_group, 
                         y = meanDiff, 
                         fill = as.character(crnSev))) +
      geom_col(position = position_dodge(preserve = "single")) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = severity.colors) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, 
                                       vjust = 1, 
                                       hjust = 1,
                                       size = rel(.9))) +
    labs(x = "Forest Type Group",
         y = "Average Biomass Loss (mg/ha)",
         fill = "Crown Burn Severity")
  
```
Figure 6. Average biomass loss across plots in each of several forest type 
groups and by plot crown burn severity, calculated as the biomass of each group 
subtracted from the average biomass in reference plots for each group. Forest 
types were calculated following Forest Inventory and Analysis guidelines, but 
using biomass as a proxy.


```{r Remove Plotting Dfs 6, include=FALSE}

rm(pData)

```

```{r Biomass Differences in Forest Type Groups and Crown Burn Severity - Write, include=FALSE}

  ggsave(filename = here("output/figures/biomassLoss_forestTypeGroupsBurnSeverity_col.jpg"),
         width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 600)

```
